title: [perf] Blocked BLAS-3 Golub-Kahan bidiag core after TSQR reject
description: Follow-up from frankenscipy-8l8r1.56. True block-TSQR tree/replay preserved reconstruction/orthogonality and public golden SHA 1cdd3658c6caef8dec9fc58fa7e12b8d5c90151e2f93df91ffe2fcf862c16225, but failed the same-process RCH score gate: bidiag_candidate_ms=75.329034, tsqr_candidate_ms=92.134037, candidate_speedup=0.817603x. Runtime route restored to the accepted public bidiag candidate. Next primitive must be fundamentally different from TSQR, QR-first, compact-WY composition, single-step fusion, replay cleanup, or thread fanout. Profile-backed target remains the 1024x512 Golub-Kahan/public SVD-family core: current restored public route on vmi1227854 measured reference_lstsq_ms=139.933797, routed_lstsq_ms=52.422991, reference_pinv_ms=154.700236, routed_pinv_ms=55.206684, while prior reduction probes still identify the bidiag core and far updates as the deeper residual. Harvested primitive: communication-avoiding dense linear algebra with BLAS-3 inner kernels from alien graveyard plus numerical-linear-algebra proof obligations from alien-artifact-coding. Required pass 1: fresh RCH baseline/reprofile first. Required lever: one safe-Rust blocked/GEMM-shaped Golub-Kahan bidiag core primitive with exact reconstruction/rank/order/sign/public-golden proof; no C BLAS/LAPACK/MKL/XLA and no unsafe. Keep only Score >= 2.0 with same-worker evidence; otherwise reject and route to the next deeper algorithm.

dependencies.title: [perf][no-gaps] Close ALL vs-upstream perf gaps in pure safe Rust — no C BLAS/LAPACK/XLA linkage
